name: Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir
name_original: Ruta del Valle Glaciar de Forcadas – circular por el Embalse de Chandrexa
region_intro: Hiking around Chandrexa De Queixa offers diverse terrain within the Central Massif of Galicia. The region features majestic mountains, notably the Serra da Queixa, with peaks exceeding 1700 meters, and ancient glacial valleys. Lush forests, numerous rivers, and a prominent reservoir contribute to the varied landscape, providing a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Chandrexa De Queixa The most popular hiking route is Matacás Viewpoint Trail, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views across the landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain and showcases the mountainous terrain. Local hikers also love the Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through a glacial valley and past the reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Chandrexa De Queixa is defined by majestic mountains, ancient glacial valleys, and lush forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Chandrexa De Queixa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 350 hikers have used komoot to explore Chandrexa De Queixa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: Chandrexa De Queixa offers a diverse network of over 15 hiking trails. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy walks to more challenging ascents through the region's mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of difficulty levels can I expect on the hiking trails in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: The trails in Chandrexa De Queixa offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find 5 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, 9 moderate trails for a good workout, and 3 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: Yes, Chandrexa De Queixa features several circular hiking routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979329460/route-of-the-glacial-valley-of-forcadas-circular-via-the-chandrexa-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a stunning glacial valley and past the reservoir. The region research also mentions the popular circular route of Celeiros, known for its natural and cultural richness.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I explore while hiking?
A: Chandrexa De Queixa is rich in natural beauty, offering majestic mountains, ancient glacial valleys, and lush forests. Hikers can discover spectacular viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena de Matacás viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive vistas. The region is also known for its dark skies, making it a great spot for stargazing from places like the Mirador Estrelas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly hiking options. The region has 5 easy routes that are suitable for families. These trails often pass through charming rural landscapes and offer a chance to enjoy nature without strenuous climbs.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in terms of historical or cultural interest?
A: Hiking routes in Chandrexa De Queixa often pass through areas with cultural and historical significance. You might encounter charming rural landscapes, including abandoned villages with traditional stone houses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4624267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vítor de Barxacova Chapel and Necropolis&lt;/a&gt; is a historical site you can visit, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from majestic mountains to serene glacial valleys, and the stunning panoramic views from various natural terraces.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Chandrexa De Queixa?
A: Chandrexa De Queixa offers diverse landscapes that change with the seasons. While specific recommendations depend on your preferred weather, the region's lush forests and shifting colors make it appealing throughout much of the year. For higher altitude trails, it's best to check local conditions, especially for snow in winter.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the region's unique glacial formations?
A: Yes, the region's ancient glacial formations are a defining feature. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979329460/route-of-the-glacial-valley-of-forcadas-circular-via-the-chandrexa-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, as it leads you directly through a glacial valley, showcasing the area's geological past.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The presence of the reservoir is a significant feature of the landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979329460/route-of-the-glacial-valley-of-forcadas-circular-via-the-chandrexa-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; passes directly by it, offering breathtaking and unforgettable panoramic views from various natural terraces.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or streams?
A: Yes, numerous waterways crisscross the landscape. The region research mentions the Queixeliña route, which traces the path of a stream, leading hikers through beautiful natural settings. You can also explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3852882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mao River Boardwalk&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Small church in the town of Requeixo (Ourense).
Reservoir formed by the waters of the Navea river with incredible natural landscapes where you can enjoy the rural atmosphere of the interior of Galicia.

waypoints.name: Church of Santa María Magdalena de Requeixo
